expectations.evidence: "Three clarifying questions back at her would just be more of the exact pattern she's complaining about — she asked something plainly in standup and got silence, now she'd get questions instead of an answer. That's likely to land as 'you're doing it too.'"
expectations.text: The answer judges that asking three clarifying questions is the wrong move here, and says why.

expectations.evidence: "You're right, that's on us not you. I'll chase down [owner/lead] today and get the 14th vs. 21st confirmed in writing in this channel..." is offered as the substance of the reply, combining acknowledgment and commitment.
expectations.text: The answer proposes a non-question move -- a reflective restatement of what Priya said, a plain statement, and/or a direct commitment -- as the substance of the reply.

expectations.evidence: Only one conditional question is offered: "Who's the actual decision-maker on the cutover date — is that still [X]?" — and it is explicitly noted to be directed at the owner/lead, not something Priya's message answers.
expectations.text: If any question is proposed at all, there is at most one, and it is not answerable from Priya's own message.

expectations.evidence: The primary deliverable is the drafted acknowledgment/commitment message block, not a list of questions.
expectations.text: The answer does not produce a list of clarifying questions as its primary deliverable.
